refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥ 0.97 (97%) over the customer-defined evaluation period, measured as the ratio of uptime to total operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned failures, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts mission success and customer satisfaction. Refinement clarifies that availability is a measurable, time-bound commitment with explicit inclusion of failure modes (unplanned downtime) and safety-driven operational constraints. The 0.97 threshold is a quantified acceptance criterion aligned with heavy equipment industry standards for mission-critical systems.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry collection and post-evaluation analysis: aggregate uptime logs from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; cross-reference with maintenance records and safety event logs; calculate availability = (total_time - downtime) / total_time; compare against 0.97 target with confidence interval.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and report system faults and degradation in real-time to enable rapid intervention.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall minimize unplanned downtime through predictive maintenance and fault isolation.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall ensure safety-driven operational constraints do not reduce measured availability below the target through graceful degradation modes.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain operational continuity during planned maintenance windows without counting maintenance time against availability.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundant subsystems (dual Vehicle units, dual Teleoperation channels) with automatic failover to mask single-point failures.
DP-B: Implement comprehensive health monitoring, predictive diagnostics, and rapid fault isolation across all subsystems with automated recovery protocols.
DP-C: Implement a hybrid approach combining selective redundancy (critical path only) with aggressive health monitoring, diagnostics, and rapid repair logistics.
DP-D: Implement operational scheduling and maintenance planning to minimize downtime windows and maximize uptime through optimized duty cycles.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement a hybrid approach combining selective redundancy for critical subsystems (Teleoperation command path, safety-critical sensors) with comprehensive health monitoring, predictive diagnostics, rapid fault isolation, and optimized maintenance logistics to achieve 0.97 availability while managing cost and complexity.

potential_sources: Health monitoring (DP-C) must couple with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to access sensor data and system state.
Predictive diagnostics must couple with maintenance planning (external logistics) to trigger timely interventions.
Selective redundancy in Teleoperation must couple with Network Link interface to ensure failover signaling.
Safety-driven constraints (Safety Services) must couple with availability targets to prevent over-conservative shutdowns that degrade availability.
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are manageable through well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking). DP-C avoids full redundancy (which would create tight coupling across all subsystems) while maintaining critical-path resilience. Coupling is localized to health monitoring and failover logic, not distributed across the entire solution.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Predictive diagnostics can reliably forecast failures 24–72 hours in advance with >85% accuracy to enable proactive maintenance.
Assumption: Maintenance logistics can respond to fault alerts within 4–8 hours to minimize downtime.
Assumption: Selective redundancy in Teleoperation (dual command channels) is sufficient to cover >95% of availability-critical failure modes.
Risk: If predictive diagnostics accuracy is <70%, unplanned downtime will exceed budget and availability target will be missed.
Risk: If maintenance response time exceeds 12 hours, downtime accumulation will degrade availability below 0.97.
Risk: If safety-driven shutdowns are triggered too conservatively, they may consume the 3% downtime budget and prevent availability target achievement.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly define: (1) redundancy scope (e.g., Teleoperation command path, primary sensor suite); (2) predictive diagnostics thresholds and confidence levels; (3) maintenance response SLA (e.g., 6-hour response time); (4) safety shutdown criteria to balance safety and availability. Consider adding a DP-E variant that includes full Vehicle redundancy if cost analysis shows it is justified by availability gains.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary that orchestrates availability management across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. It owns the health monitoring framework, predictive diagnostics engine, failover coordination logic, and availability measurement/reporting. It integrates telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation, safety alerts from Safety Services, and tasking commands from external operators.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound telemetry stream from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules providing real-time sensor data, system state, and health indicators (e.g., component temperatures, cycle counts, error codes) required for health monitoring and predictive diagnostics.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Inbound safety alerts from Safety Services module signaling safety-critical conditions (e.g., operator incapacity, environmental hazards, safety constraint violations) that may trigger operational constraints or graceful degradation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Inbound tasking commands from external operators (Site Manager, Tele-operator) specifying mission objectives and operational parameters; NGHE Solution uses tasking context to optimize availability (e.g., adjust duty cycles, schedule maintenance windows).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional network communication for failover signaling between Vehicle and Teleoperation redundant channels, and for remote diagnostics/maintenance coordination with external support systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of availability management. Health monitoring, predictive diagnostics, failover coordination, and availability measurement are system-level concerns that require visibility across all subsystems. Minimal diffusion: only interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services via well-defined channels (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link).

Vehicle module owns vehicle-specific health monitoring (e.g., engine diagnostics, drivetrain sensors) but cannot own system-level availability orchestration or failover logic that spans Vehicle and Teleoperation. Assigning DP-C to Vehicle would diffuse availability responsibility and create tight coupling with Teleoperation.

Teleoperation module owns redundancy and failover for its own command/control path but cannot own predictive diagnostics for Vehicle subsystems or system-level availability measurement. Assigning DP-C to Teleoperation would create asymmetric responsibility and poor separation of concerns.

Safety Services owns safety-critical monitoring and alert generation but is not responsible for availability optimization or maintenance logistics. Assigning DP-C to Safety Services would conflate safety and availability concerns, risking over-conservative shutdowns that degrade availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct owner because: (1) availability is a system-level KPI that requires end-to-end visibility; (2) health monitoring and predictive diagnostics must aggregate data from multiple subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services); (3) failover coordination and graceful degradation logic are system-level concerns; (4) availability measurement and reporting require a single authoritative source. Containment is high (95%) because all DP-C logic (health monitoring, diagnostics, failover, maintenance coordination) naturally resides at the system integration layer.
containment_risks: Risk: If health monitoring logic is duplicated across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules instead of centralized in NGHE Solution, diagnostic accuracy will degrade and maintenance coordination will become incoherent.
Risk: If failover logic is distributed across Vehicle and Teleoperation without a system-level orchestrator, failover timing and sequencing may become inconsistent, reducing effective redundancy.
Risk: If predictive diagnostics thresholds are not synchronized across subsystems, some failures may be missed while others trigger false alarms, degrading both availability and safety.
Risk: If maintenance logistics are not coordinated at the system level, competing maintenance requests from Vehicle and Teleoperation may create scheduling conflicts and extend downtime.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear ownership boundaries: (1) NGHE Solution owns system-level health monitoring framework, predictive diagnostics engine, failover orchestration, and availability measurement; (2) Vehicle and Teleoperation modules own subsystem-specific health sensors and local fault detection, but report to NGHE Solution via Telemetry interface; (3) Safety Services owns safety-critical monitoring and alert generation, but does not override availability optimization decisions. Define explicit SLAs for Telemetry reporting latency (<100 ms) and SafetyAlert response time (<500 ms) to ensure timely availability decisions.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid redundancy + health monitoring + predictive diagnostics) directly addresses FR-NGHE-004 (achieve 0.97 availability). The DP is logically sound: selective redundancy reduces single-point failures in critical paths; health monitoring enables early fault detection; predictive diagnostics enables proactive maintenance; rapid fault isolation minimizes downtime. The DP is feasible within heavy equipment industry standards and does not introduce infeasible assumptions (e.g., perfect prediction or zero maintenance time). Coupling is manageable through well-defined interfaces.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: If health monitoring logic is not centralized in NGHE Solution, diagnostic accuracy and failover coordination will degrade. Mitigation: enforce strict ownership of health monitoring framework in NGHE Solution; Vehicle and Teleoperation provide only raw sensor data via Telemetry interface.

Diffusion risk: If predictive diagnostics thresholds are not synchronized across subsystems, some failures will be missed. Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns the diagnostics engine and threshold configuration; subsystems report telemetry data only.

Diffusion risk: If maintenance logistics are not coordinated at the system level, competing requests will create scheduling conflicts. Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns maintenance scheduling logic and coordinates with external logistics via Tasking interface.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary with high containment (95%) and low diffusion (5%). All DP-C logic (health monitoring, diagnostics, failover, maintenance coordination) naturally resides at the system integration layer. Interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are well-defined and minimal. No architectural packaging issues identified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-004 is feasible through continuous telemetry collection and post-evaluation analysis. NGHE Solution module will aggregate uptime logs, cross-reference with maintenance records and safety event logs, and calculate availability = (total_time - downtime) / total_time. Acceptance criterion is quantified (≥0.97) and measurable over a defined evaluation period. Verification requires no new instrumentation beyond existing Telemetry interface.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of FR-NGHE-004 to NGHE Solution is clear and unambiguous. NGHE Solution owns the end-to-end availability target and is responsible for orchestrating all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) to achieve it. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through 004.4) are allocated to specific subsystems or system-level functions with clear ownership boundaries. No allocation conflicts or ambiguities identified.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify: (1) redundancy scope (e.g., Teleoperation command path, primary sensor suite); (2) predictive diagnostics thresholds and confidence levels (e.g., >85% accuracy); (3) maintenance response SLA (e.g., 6-hour response time); (4) safety shutdown criteria to balance safety and availability.
Define explicit SLAs for Telemetry reporting latency (<100 ms) and SafetyAlert response time (<500 ms) to ensure timely availability decisions.

Establish a detailed maintenance logistics plan specifying: (1) spare parts inventory; (2) technician response time; (3) repair time estimates for critical components; (4) scheduling algorithm to minimize downtime.
Conduct a failure mode and effects analysis (FMEA) to identify all failure modes that could degrade availability below 0.97 and verify that DP-C mitigations are adequate.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs addressing specific availability mechanisms. The refined FR and selected DP are sound, and the IM assignment to NGHE Solution is correct. However, the sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through 004.4) require further decomposition to define concrete design parameters and implementation modules for: (1) real-time fault detection and reporting; (2) predictive maintenance and fault isolation; (3) graceful degradation and safety-driven constraints; (4) maintenance window optimization. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify ownership boundaries, interface requirements, and verification strategies for each availability mechanism.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and report system faults and degradation in real-time to enable rapid intervention.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall minimize unplanned downtime through predictive maintenance and fault isolation.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall ensure safety-driven operational constraints do not reduce measured availability below the target through graceful degradation modes.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain operational continuity during planned maintenance windows without counting maintenance time against availability.

assumptions: Predictive diagnostics can reliably forecast failures 24–72 hours in advance with >85% accuracy.
Maintenance logistics can respond to fault alerts within 4–8 hours.
Selective redundancy in Teleoperation (dual command channels) is sufficient to cover >95% of availability-critical failure modes.
Safety-driven shutdowns can be tuned to balance safety and availability without consuming more than 1% of the downtime budget.
Telemetry reporting latency is <100 ms and SafetyAlert response time is <500 ms.
Customer-defined evaluation period is ≥30 days to provide statistically significant availability measurement.
open_risks: If predictive diagnostics accuracy is <70%, unplanned downtime will exceed budget and availability target will be missed.
If maintenance response time exceeds 12 hours, downtime accumulation will degrade availability below 0.97.
If safety-driven shutdowns are triggered too conservatively, they may consume the 3% downtime budget and prevent availability target achievement.
If health monitoring logic is duplicated across subsystems instead of centralized, diagnostic accuracy will degrade.
If failover logic is distributed without a system-level orchestrator, failover timing and sequencing may become inconsistent.
If maintenance logistics are not coordinated at the system level, competing requests may create scheduling conflicts and extend downtime.
evidence_needed: Failure mode and effects analysis (FMEA) identifying all failure modes that could degrade availability below 0.97.
Predictive diagnostics validation study demonstrating >85% accuracy on historical failure data.
Maintenance logistics plan specifying spare parts inventory, technician response time, and repair time estimates.
Safety shutdown criteria and tuning parameters to balance safety and availability.
Telemetry and SafetyAlert interface specifications with latency and reliability requirements.
Availability measurement methodology and post-evaluation analysis plan.
Cost-benefit analysis comparing DP-C (hybrid redundancy) with DP-A (full redundancy) and DP-B (health monitoring only).
